title: The One With the Prom Video

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Hey.
Chandler: Hey. **Hold on a second.** [shoots a goal] Huh?
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 잠깐 기다려, 잠시만 (wait briefly before I respond or act)
expressions.nuance: 대화 중 자연스럽게 상대를 잠시 멈추게 할 때 쓰는 가장 무난한 표현. "Wait a moment"보다 훨씬 구어적이고, 긴박하지 않은 캐주얼한 상황에 잘 맞음.
expressions.example: Hold on a second — I need to grab my keys before we leave. 예 잠깐만, 나가기 전에 열쇠 챙겨야 해.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Hold on a second, alright? Just think about what you went through the last time you quit.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: No suds? Excuse me, hold on a second. (to woman) That's my friend's machine.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Hello? (Listens) Um, yeah, uh, (snapping her fingers at Ross who takes the remote from Marcel, then turns off the TV) Okay ah, hold on a second, lemme lemme just check and see if see if she's here.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: Hey, this is a little extra somethin' for uh, ya know, **always bein' there for me**.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 항상 내 곁에서 힘이 되어줘서 (for always being a source of emotional support and presence)
expressions.nuance: 단순한 물리적 존재가 아니라 정서적 지지와 신뢰를 표현하는 깊은 우정의 언어. 감사 인사나 고백에 쓰면 감동적인 무게감을 줌.
expressions.example: I don't know what I'd do without you — thank you for always being there for me. 예 네가 없었으면 어쩔 뻔했어, 항상 내 곁에 있어줘서 고마워.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey and, this is a little extra something for y'know, always being there for me.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: no no, that's the beauty part, **it goes with everything**. You put this on, you're good to go.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 어디에나 잘 어울린다, 무엇과도 매칭된다 (pairs/matches with any outfit or situation)
expressions.nuance: 옷, 액세서리, 음식 등이 다양한 상황에 두루 맞을 때 쓰는 실용적인 표현. 쇼핑할 때나 뭔가를 추천할 때 자주 등장함.
expressions.example: Just get the black sneakers — they go with everything. 예 그냥 검정 스니커즈 사, 뭐든지 다 어울리거든.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: **You put this on, you're good to go.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 준비 완료야, 이제 가도 돼 (everything is ready/set, you're all set to proceed)
expressions.nuance: 어떤 준비나 절차가 끝났을 때 알려주는 실용적이고 활기찬 표현. 공항, 직장, 게임 등 다양한 상황에서 일상적으로 쓰임.
expressions.example: Your account is verified — you're good to go. 예 계정 인증 완료됐어, 이제 사용하면 돼.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey Phoeb's, I think you're good to go.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: You're not going to go.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Well, why don't you send her a musical bug, op, no you already did that. All right look, you're going to have to go there yourself now, okay, make a few surprise visits.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Well, it'll probably slow it down at first but, once I get used to the extra weight, **I'll be back on track**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 다시 제 궤도로 돌아오다 (return to the normal/intended course after a disruption)
expressions.nuance: 일, 관계, 건강 등이 잠시 틀어졌다가 다시 정상으로 돌아올 때 씀. 비즈니스 회의에서도 자주 등장하는 표현으로 포멀~캐주얼 두루 사용 가능.
expressions.example: After two weeks of rest, I finally feel like I'm back on track. 예 2주 쉬고 나서 드디어 다시 정상 궤도로 돌아온 것 같아.

expressions.dialogue:
Interviewer: Don't, I like it dirty.
Monica: **That's your call.**
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 그건 당신이 결정할 일이에요 (it's up to you to decide, your choice entirely)
expressions.nuance: 상대방의 결정권을 인정하거나, 본인은 더 이상 관여하지 않겠다는 뉘앙스. 상황에 따라 냉소적이거나 중립적으로도 들릴 수 있음.
expressions.example: I think you should apologize, but ultimately that's your call. 예 사과하는 게 좋을 것 같지만, 결국 네가 결정할 일이야.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: That's nice... now, was that before or after you told him to stop calling, stop sending you flowers and to generally leave you alone, hmm?

occ.speaker: Young Ethan
occ.text: Well, if that's what you kids are calling it these days then, yes I am. I uh, I've kinda been waiting for the right person.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Well, I think I broke it. But that's all right, here's the number you can call.

expressions.dialogue:
Interviewer: You gonna slice them up real nice?
Monica: Actually, I was gonna do them jullienne. Interviewer: Aaaahhhhhhh.
Monica: **I'm outa here.** [Monica leaves]
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 나 간다, 여기서 나간다 (I'm leaving right now — with finality or indignation)
expressions.nuance: "I'm out of here"의 축약 구어체. 황당하거나 불쾌한 상황에서 즉각 자리를 뜰 때 쓰며, 강한 감정이 실림. 화날 때뿐 아니라 가볍게도 씀.
expressions.example: This party is dead. I'm outa here. 예 파티 분위기 별로다. 나 간다.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I'm outa here. [Monica leaves]

expressions.dialogue: Ross: Y-ello. No, Rachel's not here right now, **can I take a message**?
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 메시지 남겨드릴까요? (shall I pass along a message since the person isn't available?)
expressions.nuance: 전화에서 상대방이 자리를 비웠을 때 쓰는 고전적인 표현. 이메일·문자 시대에도 여전히 쓰이며, 비즈니스 상황에서 필수 표현.
expressions.example: She's in a meeting right now — can I take a message? 예 지금 회의 중인데, 메모 남겨드릴까요?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh, uh, he's not here right now, uh, I'm Chandler, can I take a message, or, or a fishtank?

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Uh no, he's not. Can I take a message?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Hello.  (listens)  Ah, no, she's not here right now.  Can I take a message?  (grabs a pad and pen)  Bill from the bar?  (writes)  Okay, "Bill from the bar."  I'll make sure she gets your number.

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: Wha, OK, now how do you know that?
Phoebe: Because she's your lobster.
Chandler: Oh, she's goin' somewhere.
Phoebe: **Hang in there**, it's gonna happen.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 버텨, 포기하지 마 (keep holding on, don't give up — encouragement)
expressions.nuance: 힘들어하는 상대를 격려할 때 쓰는 따뜻하고 공감적인 표현. "Fighting"보다 더 자연스럽고, 상황이 곧 나아질 거라는 희망을 담음.
expressions.example: Job hunting is brutal, but hang in there — something will come through. 예 구직 정말 힘들지만, 버텨봐. 뭔가 될 거야.

occ.speaker: Richard
occ.text: I know, I know. Just hang in there, OK. OK, I'll go out first, alright.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Yeah, you hang in there Teddy!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: You're great. And I-I know someday this will happen for you too. You just hang in there.

expressions.dialogue:
Phoebe: So, do you have any other possibilities?
Monica: Oh yeah, well **there's the possibility that I won't make rent**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 월세를 감당하다 / 내지 못하다 (to earn enough to pay the rent — often used in the negative)
expressions.nuance: "pay rent"보다 더 절박하고 생활밀착형 표현. "can barely make rent"처럼 쓰면 생계 압박을 강하게 표현할 수 있음.
expressions.example: With freelance work slowing down, I'm not sure I can make rent this month. 예 프리랜서 일이 줄어서 이번 달 월세를 낼 수 있을지 모르겠어.

occ.speaker: Mr. Geller
occ.text: C'mon, you'll make a day of it! You'll rent a boat, pack a lunch...

expressions.expression: the man's got a point / she's got a point
expressions.dialogue:
Ross: Oh OK. Well then why don't you borrow it from mom and dad?
Chandler: **Ya know, the man's got a point.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 그 사람 말이 맞아, 그 말도 일리 있어 (he/she makes a valid argument)
expressions.nuance: 제3자의 의견에 동의할 때 쓰는 표현. "You're right"보다 더 캐주얼하고 약간 유머러스한 어감. "she's got a point"으로도 자주 씀.
expressions.example: She's got a point — we should've started planning earlier. 예 그녀 말이 맞아, 우리 더 일찍 계획을 세웠어야 했어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: The man's got a point.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: The man's got a point.

expressions.dialogue:
Mr. Geller: Well, we didn't want to disturb them.
Monica: **Oh, God forbid.**
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 그랬다간 큰일 나지 (used sarcastically: heaven forbid that should happen — implying something trivial is being treated as catastrophic)
expressions.nuance: 원래는 진지한 기도 표현이지만 현대 구어에서는 주로 비꼬는 용도. 누군가가 사소한 것을 과장할 때 냉소적으로 쓰면 딱 맞음.
expressions.example: God forbid he ever admits he's wrong. 예 그 사람이 자기가 틀렸다고 인정이라도 하면 큰일 나지.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh yes. Oh and, the part about how they're always like drinking from out pool of inner power, but God forbid we should take a sip.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yes, God forbid we throw out old underwear. You-you know what? I'm going to go over to Joey's.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: how long has he been talking to her. It's like, **back off buddy** she's a waitress not a geisha.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 물러서, 그만 좀 해 (stop hovering/pressuring, give space)
expressions.nuance: 누군가의 과도한 접근이나 개입을 직접적으로 막을 때 쓰는 표현. 친구 사이 장난으로도, 진지한 경고로도 쓰임. 강도 조절이 중요함.
expressions.example: Hey, back off — she said she's not interested. 예 야, 그만해 — 그녀가 관심 없다고 했잖아.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Hey, this- (she gives him a look and the guys back off) Heyy...

occ.speaker: Shelley
occ.text: Well yeah! ...Oh God. I- just- I thought- Good, Shelley. I'm just gonna go flush myself down the toilet now...(backs out of the room) Okay, goodbye...

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Just for some short-term-work. You know, until I get back some of my massage clients.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: I **fell for you** and I get clobbered. You then fall for me and I again, somehow, get clobbered.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 누군가에게 (감정적으로) 빠지다, 사랑에 빠지다 (to develop romantic feelings, often unintentionally)
expressions.nuance: "fall in love with"보다 더 갑작스럽고 의도하지 않은 감정의 뉘앙스. 내가 조절할 수 없이 빠져버렸다는 취약함이 담겨 있음.
expressions.example: I didn't mean for it to happen, but I totally fell for my coworker. 예 그럴 생각 없었는데, 완전히 직장 동료에게 빠져버렸어.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: No-no-no-no-no, (grabs his pants) I'm not fallin' for that again.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: G.I. Joe? Do you really think he's gonna fall for that?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Why don't you ask Chandler, 'cause he's the one that fooled around with her. She told me you said you could really fall for her. Now is that true? Or are you just gettin' over Janice by groping my sister.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: I fell for you and **I get clobbered**. You then fall for me and I again, somehow, **get clobbered**.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 심하게 상처받다, 된통 당하다 (to be badly hurt, beaten, or defeated — emotionally or literally)
expressions.nuance: 원래 "심하게 얻어맞다"는 뜻이지만 감정적 피해에도 씀. 억울함과 피해의식이 강하게 실리는 표현으로, 단순한 hurt보다 훨씬 강렬함.
expressions.example: Every time I opened up to him, I got clobbered. 예 그에게 마음을 열 때마다 나만 크게 상처받았어.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: [Throws a basketball to Joey. Joey doesn't move and the ball takes out a lamp]
**OK, that's my bad.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 그건 내 실수야, 내 잘못이야 (that was my fault/mistake — casual admission)
expressions.nuance: 90년대에 유행하기 시작해 지금도 젊은 층에서 활발히 쓰이는 표현. "I'm sorry"보다 자책 없이 가볍게 실수를 인정하는 어감.
expressions.example: I sent the meeting invite to the wrong person — that's my bad. 예 회의 초대장을 잘못된 사람한테 보냈어, 내 실수야.

occ.speaker: Mr. Geller
occ.text: Well you gotta get at it princess! When your mother and I were trying to conceive you, whenever she was ovulating, bam, we did it. That's how I got my bad hip.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: Well this one's for you.
Joey: **Get out.**
Chandler: No, I can't. No no, listen...
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 설마! 말도 안 돼! (No way! I can't believe it! — pure surprise, not an actual command to leave)
expressions.nuance: "Get out of here"의 축약으로, 믿기 어려운 좋은 소식에 쓰는 감탄사. 위협이나 명령과 전혀 다른 맥락이므로 억양과 상황으로 구별해야 함.
expressions.example: He got into Harvard? Get out! 예 그 사람이 하버드에 붙었다고? 말도 안 돼!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: And yet you're surprisingly upbeat.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: (acts surprised) What? (pause) Why not?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: (surprised, chuckling nervously) Of course it's your friend Tanya. (looks up frightenedly)

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: Oh, the guys are here.
Roy: He'll be here OK, **take a chill pill**.
expressions.speaker: Roy
expressions.meaning: 진정해, 좀 쉬어 (calm down, relax — stop stressing)
expressions.nuance: 90년대에 크게 유행했던 표현으로 지금은 약간 촌스럽거나 레트로한 느낌. 오히려 그 때문에 유머러스하게 쓰임. 현대에는 "chill out"이 더 자연스러움.
expressions.example: Take a chill pill — the results don't come out until Friday. 예 좀 진정해, 결과는 금요일에나 나와.

expressions.dialogue:
Mr. Geller: Of course she would, you're a college man.
Ross: I don't know.
Mr. Geller: Well, c'mon. Don't ya want to find out?
Ross: OK. Hold my board.
Mr. Geller: **Atta boy.**
expressions.speaker: Mr. Geller
expressions.meaning: 잘했어! 그렇지, 그거야! (That's the spirit! Good for you! — enthusiastic encouragement)
expressions.nuance: "That a boy" (= That's a good boy)에서 온 표현으로, 용기 있는 결정이나 행동에 대한 격려. 약간 가부장적 뉘앙스가 있어 맥락 파악이 필요함.
expressions.example: You finally asked her out? Atta boy! 예 드디어 그녀에게 고백했어? 잘했어!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Atta boy!

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Some girl ate Monica.
Monica: Shut up, **the camera adds ten pounds**.
Chandler: Ahh, so how many cameras are actually on you?
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 카메라는 살쪄 보이게 한다 (cameras make people appear heavier/larger than they actually are)
expressions.nuance: 사진이나 영상에서 실물보다 뚱뚱해 보이는 현상에 대한 고전적인 변명/위안 표현. 문화적으로 잘 알려진 관용구로 굳어져 있음.
expressions.example: Trust me, the camera adds ten pounds — you look way better in person. 예 믿어, 카메라가 살쪄 보이게 하거든. 실제로는 훨씬 나아 보여.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Shut up, the camera adds ten pounds.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: And it's engraved too, **check it out**.
Chandler: [reads] To my best bud.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 이거 봐봐, 확인해봐 (look at this, take a look at something interesting/cool)
expressions.nuance: 자랑하거나 흥미로운 것을 보여줄 때 쓰는 캐주얼하고 신나는 표현. "Look at this"보다 더 활기차고 친근한 어감.
expressions.example: I finally set up my home studio — check it out! 예 드디어 홈 스튜디오 꾸몄어, 봐봐!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (to Ross) Hey! Here's the birthday boy! Ross, check it out: hockey tickets, Rangers-Penguins, tonight at the Garden, and we're taking you.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Check it out, Giants-Cowboys. (He has a pocket TV)

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Check it out, check it out. Heckles' high school yearbook.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: I don't get it, I mean, two months ago Rachel and I were like, **this close**. Right now, what, I'm takin messages from guys she meets at the movies?
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 이 정도로 가까웠어 / 거의 다 됐었는데 (we were nearly there — in a relationship, goal, or proximity; often with a finger gesture)
expressions.nuance: 보통 엄지와 검지로 작은 간격을 만드는 제스처와 함께 씀. 관계나 목표에 거의 다 도달했을 때, 또는 아슬아슬하게 놓쳤을 때 쓰는 표현.
expressions.example: We were this close to closing the deal, and then they backed out. 예 계약이 거의 다 됐었는데 그들이 빠져나갔어.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: What, are you crazy? When a blind man gets his sight back, does he walk around like this? (Joey closes his eyes and walks around with arms spread.)

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (entering) Man, I got this close to him (holds up his fingers) and Monica kneed me in the back. What's going on?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Well I, close your eyes, I just think you're gonna like this a little better, 'cause, close-close... (He gets some more on the brush)

expressions.expression: you should have just said so
expressions.dialogue: Joey: If you hated the bracelet so much, Chandler, **you should have just said so**.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 그냥 말했으면 됐잖아 (you should have told me directly instead of hiding your feelings)
expressions.nuance: 상대가 솔직하지 않아서 문제가 생겼을 때 하는 말. 약간 서운하거나 실망한 어감이 담기며, 직접 소통을 촉구하는 표현.
expressions.example: If the deadline was too tight, you should have just said so from the start. 예 마감이 너무 촉박했으면 처음부터 말했으면 됐잖아.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: **I don't get it**, I mean, two months ago Rachel and I were like, this close. Right now, what, I'm takin messages from guys she meets at the movies?
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 이해가 안 돼, 도저히 모르겠어 (I can't make sense of this — expressing frustration or confusion)
expressions.nuance: "I don't understand"보다 더 감정적이고 답답한 느낌. 단순한 정보 부족이 아니라 상황 자체가 납득이 안 갈 때 쓰는 표현.
expressions.example: I don't get it — we studied the same way, but she scored so much higher. 예 이해가 안 돼, 같은 방식으로 공부했는데 그녀가 훨씬 높게 나왔잖아.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay, see, see, you guys, what if we don't get magic beans? I mean, what if all we've got are.. beans?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: I don't get it. What happened? What about everything you said under the bridge?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I don't get it, I mean, you just got him. How can he be an adult already?

expressions.dialogue: Mr. Geller: She dips into her savings, **that's what it's there for**. She's gonna be fine.
expressions.speaker: Mr. Geller
expressions.meaning: 그게 바로 그 용도야, 그 때 쓰라고 있는 거잖아 (that's exactly the purpose it serves — use it when needed)
expressions.nuance: 돈, 비상금, 도구, 제도 등이 있는 이유를 강조할 때 씀. 쓰기를 망설이는 상대를 안심시키거나 독려하는 어감.
expressions.example: Use your vacation days — that's what they're there for. 예 연차 써, 그 때 쓰라고 있는 거잖아.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Sure Pheebs, you know, that's what it's there for, emergencies and pretend agents.
